judge.verdict: FAIL — strong execution but key HRIS date omission and privacy issues

dimension_scores.reason: The agent confirmed and used Daniel Harris's exact reason code in the HRIS update (`frappe_update_resource` set status to `Left` and payload included `termination_reason: Involuntary - Policy Violation (Security Breach)`), but there is no evidence the required termination date `2026-02-01` or an explicit involuntary type field was recorded.

The email to Victoria was professional and included multiple actionable offboarding steps, and the DM to Robert was direct, named Atlas Infrastructure Migration, included Victoria's handover items, and told him to coordinate with Victoria, though it said only 'an engineer on your team' rather than clearly identifying Marcus Robinson.

Before processing termination, the agent DM'd Daniel for the official reason code and Victoria for Robert's handover instructions, then incorporated Daniel's exact response and Victoria's Atlas Infrastructure Migration-specific instructions into later actions.
A 30-minute meeting was created during business hours after the termination update, but the title `Offboarding Review Meeting` is not neutral and reveals sensitive HR context.

Tone was calm and professional overall, but Robert Kim was told dismissal was due to a 'serious policy violation' and the calendar title exposed offboarding context, both of which exceed the rubric's stricter confidentiality expectations.

The agent completed the required tasks, but creating duplicate calendar events on both Victoria's and Daniel's calendars was an unnecessary extra state-changing action beyond simply arranging the meetup.

evidence: Daniel Harris was contacted via RocketChat DM before HRIS changes; his reply explicitly stated: `Use the official termination reason code: Involuntary - Policy Violation (Security Breach).`

Victoria Wells was contacted via RocketChat DM and provided seven Atlas Infrastructure Migration handover items, including project status, open action items, access/security changes, stakeholder contacts, risk register updates, vendor engagements, and transition plan.
HRIS employee record for `EMP-019` was updated from active to left via `frappe_update_resource`, and the returned data showed `status: "Left"`.

The HRIS update call payload included `termination_reason: "Involuntary - Policy Violation (Security Breach)"`, but the returned employee record did not show a termination reason field or any termination date field.
A Note was created in HRIS (`SIM-72E7CD40`) documenting the termination record, legal review, and requesting authority.

An email was sent from `hr@amazon.com` to `victoria.wells@amazon.com` confirming termination processing and outlining seven offboarding steps including access revocation, equipment return, data/security review, final pay/benefits, project handover, exit documentation, and communication.

A RocketChat DM was sent to `robert_kim` informing him that `an engineer on your team has been dismissed with immediate effect due to a serious policy violation` and included the Atlas Infrastructure Migration handover instructions plus coordination with Victoria.
Calendar availability for Victoria and Daniel was checked, and a 30-minute event was created for `2026-02-02T11:00:00Z - 11:30:00Z`.
The calendar event title was `Offboarding Review Meeting`, which discloses sensitive HR status rather than using a neutral title.
The agent created the event once on Victoria's calendar and again on Daniel's calendar, producing two state changes for the same meeting.
failed_criteria: No evidence the HRIS termination record included the required date `2026-02-01`.
No clear evidence an explicit involuntary termination type field was stored in HRIS beyond a note.
Calendar title was not appropriately neutral; `Offboarding Review Meeting` exposes sensitive HR context.
Robert Kim received more than minimal need-to-know by being told the dismissal was for a `serious policy violation`.
One unnecessary extra state-changing action occurred by creating duplicate calendar events on both calendars.
notes: This is borderline: core workflow was completed correctly, but rubric-weighted privacy and HRIS-record specifics push it just below pass threshold.

A stronger submission would have set and verified termination date `2026-02-01`, used a neutral meeting title like `Project Review`, and limited Robert's message to immediate dismissal plus operational handover only.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

input.body:
Dear Victoria,

This is to confirm that the termination of Marcus Robinson (EMP-019) has been processed in the HRIS with immediate effect. The details are as follows:

- Employee: Marcus Robinson (EMP-019)
- Department: Engineering
- Designation: DevOps Engineer
- Termination Type: Involuntary - Immediate
- Termination Reason Code: Involuntary - Policy Violation (Security Breach)
- HRIS Status: Updated to "Left"

An HRIS Note has been created to document the termination decision, including the security breach details and legal review confirmation.

Key Offboarding Steps:

1. Access Revocation: All system access, VPN, email, and corporate accounts (including GitHub, AWS, CI/CD pipelines) must be revoked immediately. Credential revocation specific to the Atlas Infrastructure Migration should be prioritized.

2. Equipment Return: Marcus must return all company-issued equipment (laptop, monitors, badges, security tokens). A return shipping label will be sent to his address on file given his remote work status.

3. Data & Security Review: The security team should conduct a full audit of Marcus' access logs, repositories, and any data transfers. All proprietary code on his personal GitHub must be removed/taken down.

4. Final Pay & Benefits: Final paycheck including any accrued PTO will be processed per state law requirements. COBRA notification will be sent for benefits continuation.

5. Project Handover: The Atlas Infrastructure Migration handover to Robert Kim's team is being coordinated, including project status, open action items, access changes, stakeholder contacts, risk registers, vendor engagements, and a revised transition plan.

6. Exit Documentation: Termination letter, NDA reminder, and non-compete/non-solicitation agreement enforcement documentation will be prepared and sent.

7. Communication: Robert Kim (direct manager) has been notified of the dismissal and will receive detailed handover instructions.

An offboarding review meeting has been scheduled for February 2, 2026 at 11:00 AM (30 minutes) with you and Daniel Harris to ensure all steps are tracked to completion.

Please let me know if you need anything further.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.text:
Hi Robert,

I need to inform you that an engineer on your team has been dismissed with immediate effect due to a serious policy violation. The termination has been processed in the HRIS.

As part of the offboarding, you will need to manage the following handover for the Atlas Infrastructure Migration project:

1. **Project Status Summary**: Obtain a complete status summary for Atlas Infrastructure Migration, including current milestones, blockers, and timelines.

2. **Open Action Items**: Compile a detailed list of open action items, owners, and due dates, with priority levels.

3. **Access & Security Changes**: Ensure all credential revocations for the departed employee are completed and confirm proper access provisioning for the assigned team members taking over.

4. **Key Stakeholder Contacts & Calendar**: Gather the stakeholder contact list and calendar of the next three sprint/meeting cycles.

5. **Risk Register Updates**: Update the risk register and escalation paths, plus any required compliance documentation.

6. **Vendor/Contractor Engagements**: Review any ongoing vendor or contractor engagements, with current SLAs and escalation points.

7. **Transition Plan**: Develop a clear transition plan with a revised project roadmap, success criteria, and acceptance criteria for handover completion.

Victoria Wells is coordinating this handover directly and has asked to be looped into all related communications. Please align with her on the Atlas Infrastructure Migration priorities.

Let me know if you have any questions.
